verdict: An explicit, calibrated uncertainty read out of the grid population (the reconstruction residual = inter-module inconsistency) rises with path integration, resets at a cue, and is calibrated to the true decode error — but ONLY under independent module drift (under shared drift the code is confidently wrong). That uncertainty drives emergent Bayesian cue re-weighting (the reliability law agent_cue_integration.py left open) and a re-anchor switch that follows the BELIEF, not the truth. Reliability-blind controls fail all three.
